title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:
Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

Join us this Friday from 5-7 PM as we welcome Vasen for our free tasting! Scroll down to see our full tasting line-up for May.

👻Parish with all the ghosts!
• Ghost in the Machine DIPA with Citra
• DDH Ghost in the Machine DIPA with Citra

✨ The Veil newbies
• Fracture 3 West Coast IPA with Mosaic Dynaboost, Simcoe & Krush
• IREALLYdontwanttoBU DDH Hazy IPA with Citra & Mosaic
• Never Calm Gose with passion fruit & pineapple

🫰 New drop from Benchtop
• Disturbance in the Schwartz Foeder Aged Schwartzbier
• Soft Crash DDH IPA with Mosaic
• West Coast Proven Theory West Coast IPA with Cascade, Mosaic & Citra

😍Krushing on Commonwealth
• One Night in Prague Czech-Style Lager
• Kandy Krush Hazy DIPA with Nelson Sauvin, Krush & Krush Lupocore

😮‍💨 Schilling making us very thirsty
• Hanse Keller Pils
• Zappes Kolsch

👏 Welcome back Charm City!
• Elderberry Mead
• Wildflower Mead
• Original Dry Mead
• Strawberry Ginger Mead

🚗 Euro trippin’
• St. Feuillien Grand Cru Belgian Strong
• Victory Prima Light German Pilsner

🌟Lost Generation Glow
• Love Umma Korean-style Lager
• Mango Sunrise Hefeweizen with Alphonso mango
• Asian Glow 2.0 fruited sour with dragon fruit, Alphonso mango, passion fruit & plum (High Side collab!)

👌🏻Charles Towne Fermentory crispies
• Carolina Cream Ale
• Estereo Mexican Lager

🍹 Get your cocktail on
• Cutwater Vodka Transfusion
• Vasen Margarita Sour

💭 Dreaming of Dewey
• Single & Swishy Hazy IPA
• Secret Machine Rainbow Sherbet Sour
• Secret Machine Strawberry Pretzel Salad Sour

👟New kicks, new beers from Burnish
• Northwood West Coast IPA
• Peach Socks Wheat ale with peach
• New Kicks Hazy IPA with Citra Cryo, Nectaron & Galaxy

🎧Drop a beat for Dancing Gnome
• Hefeweizen
• Mexican Lager
• Side of House IPA with Centennial, Cascade, Citra, Amarillo & Mosaic
• Goblin Folk IPA with Simcoe & Columbus
• Double Lustra Hazy DIPA with Citra & Amarillo
• Lustra Hazy Pale Ale with Citra
• Strata Jam IPA with Strata

🌞 Weekend ready
• Shiner Summer Shandy Variety Pack
• Avery Yuzilla Golden Ale with yuzu
• Solace Sun Kissed Summer Ale

🙌 Other Half Thank You for Juicing DDH Imperial IPA with Citra, Nelson Sauvin, Citra Cryo & Sabro Cryo

 Fruit is healthy right?
• Blue Mountain Raspberries on Acid Sour Witbier with Raspberries
• Hardywood Virginia Blackberry Witbier

 Sippin’ on summer
• Starr Hill Sun Burn Summer Wheat
• Troegs Field Study IPA with Citra, Mosaic & El Dorado

⭕️ Freshies from Adroit Theory
• Culture of Complaint Lager
• Circle of Hell Hazy IPA with Citra, Nelson Sauvin & Mosaic
• Necropolis 2034 AD Hazy Triple IPA with Citra, Motueka & El Dorado

*Expected Thursday*

Benchtop Dolcita Brochachosaurus Hazy IPA

The Answer Ride 2 Richmond Hazy IPA

Brainstorm Rio de Oro Mexican Lager

New wines in the door!

Sophie Schaal Alsace Riesling: Sophie Schaal brings us a classic Alsace Riesling - check out Julie’s wine write-up below for all the details!

Domaine de Chantemerle Chablis: Grandpa Boudin was a cooper, but his son hated the cooperage business so when he started making Chablis he refused to use barrels. His son, Francis, carries on the tradition of "barrel free Chablis" to allow for the purest expression of Chardonnay. Lively lemon and citrus pair with white flowers and a chalky minerality, finished with just the right amount of texture. Practicing sustainable, hand harvested, native yeast, unfiltered, unfined.

Chateau de Crezancy Sancerre: Coming from a family-run estate, Crezancy Sancerre is one of only a few château-bottled Sancerres in existence. Elegant and classic with crisp citrus, lingering minerality and a touch of white pepper on the finish.

Coste di Brenta Trebbiano d'Abruzzo: A family vineyard, all of the wine from Coste di Brenta is hand-crafted and organically made. This Trebbiano is beautifully textured, lively and oh so easy to drink with notes of grapefruit and apple. You'll find me buying one, two, or three... bottles for myself.

Chateau La Genestière "Les Galets" Rose: Fresh and vibrant, Les Galets comes from a southern France estate located just outside the Rose powerhouse region of Tavel. A beautiful salmon pink in color, Les Galets hits first with bright red fruit and floral notes, followed quickly by a firmly balanced minerality. Nuance abounds in this excellent value French crusher. Sustainable.

Colombaio Chianti Classico: Our favorite Italian-focused distributor, Alek, pointed us to this loveable Classico. Colombaio di Cencio was born in 1993 when German entrepreneur Werner Ernst Wilhelm was so captivated by the beauty of Chianti that he transformed a historic country house into a winery. Bursting with cherry, red currant and violet with a soft, fresh finish.

 

Sophie Schaal Alsace Riesling: Sophie Schaal brings us a classic Alsace Riesling - check out Julie’s wine write-up below for all the details!

Domaine de Chantemerle Chablis: Grandpa Boudin was a cooper, but his son hated the cooperage business so when he started making Chablis he refused to use barrels. His son, Francis, carries on the tradition of "barrel free Chablis" to allow for the purest expression of Chardonnay. Lively lemon and citrus pair with white flowers and a chalky minerality, finished with just the right amount of texture. Practicing sustainable, hand harvested, native yeast, unfiltered, unfined.

Chateau de Crezancy Sancerre: Coming from a family-run estate, Crezancy Sancerre is one of only a few château-bottled Sancerres in existence. Elegant and classic with crisp citrus, lingering minerality and a touch of white pepper on the finish.

This Trebbiano is beautifully textured, lively and oh so easy to drink with notes of grapefruit and apple. You'll find me buying one, two, or three... bottles for myself.

A beautiful salmon pink in color, Les Galets hits first with bright red fruit and floral notes, followed quickly by a firmly balanced minerality. Nuance abounds in this excellent value French crusher. Sustainable.

Colombaio Chianti Classico: Our favorite Italian-focused distributor, Alek, pointed us to this loveable Classico. Colombaio di Cencio was born in 1993 when German entrepreneur Werner Ernst Wilhelm was so captivated by the beauty of Chianti that he transformed a historic country house into a winery. Bursting with cherry, red currant and violet with a soft, fresh finish.

I bet you're shocked to see a wine writeup on an Alsatian White and it's not coming from Beth. Yes, I stole her thunder on this one because, well, it's just that good.

Sophie Schaal was born in Burgundy, but she credits South Africa with really lighting the fire of her wine journey, as it was the place that broadened her horizons and helped her to take her oenology degree to the next level. Today she splits time between France and South Africa and makes some banging wines from both regions.

Sophie Schaal Riesling is classic Alsace. If you need an intro to Alsatian whites, start here. Notes of green apple, citrus, slate, and minerality are all hallmarks of Alsatian Riesling but the zesty acidity is what makes the region famous. Sophie Schaal lets all those classic notes shine in this intensely aromatic and wonderfully dry Riesling. We've had plenty of dry Riesling here in the shop, but Sophie Schaal grabbed my attention in a way many others don't. Nuanced and complex while also being so damn easy to drink - this bottle is for everyone.

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Julie.

May Tasting Events are spring ready!

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
